message:
fix(engine): seqlock fastpath — panic safety via SequencePublishGuard and poison recovery

If the user-supplied on_odd_sequence hook panicked during publish, the
sequence counter was left at an odd value permanently, causing all future
read_clone_or_else calls to fall back forever (readers see odd = write in
progress). Additionally, poisoned Mutex/RwLock guards caused hard panics
on subsequent operations.

Changes:
- Add SequencePublishGuard with a Drop impl that unconditionally stores
  the final (even) sequence number, ensuring the publish completes even
  if the hook panics — readers see the pre-panic snapshot, and the
  next publish succeeds normally
- Normalize odd start sequences: if a publish begins with an already-odd
  sequence (from a prior panic), treat it as writer pressure rather than
  incrementing into an invalid state
- Add lock_writer_gate(), snapshot_read(), snapshot_write() helpers that
  recover from poisoned locks via into_inner() instead of propagating
  panic — the seqlock protocol tolerates stale snapshots, so poison
  recovery is safe here
- Remove all .expect() calls on lock acquisition

Tests:
- panicking_publish_hook_does_not_brick_future_publishes: verify sequence
  recovery, stale snapshot visibility, and successful subsequent publish
- odd_start_publish_normalizes_to_writer_pressure_then_even_commit:
  verify read falls back during write, final sequence is even

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
